since_last: Since 10:30 AM: SpaceX crossed at $162.81 and trades $169.46 (+25.5%) — the perp's ~$170 handicap was right almost to the dollar; semis stretched the morning leg while NVDA went flat; ADBE −6.7% on a CFO-departure headline.

cards.headline: SPCX: $2.2T debut, and the perp market called it
cards.body: SpaceX opened at $162.81 vs the $135 IPO price after pre-open indications walked down from $175 to $151, and now trades $169.46 (+25.5%), a ~$2.2T market cap. Hyperliquid's pre-IPO perp converged to spot within dollars ($169.2) — the crypto venue priced this debut better than the indication tape. Cboe lists SPCX options Tuesday.
cards.watch: A first close above the $162.81 opening print keeps the debut bid intact; $135 is the IPO floor below.

cards.headline: Semis push a third leg — without NVDA
cards.body: SOXX +2.26% and SMH +2.09% against SPY +0.48% (12:55 PM ET, ~15-min delayed); Chips & Compute +2.57% leads, Equipment +2.12% behind it. INTC +7.0%, ARM +8.8%, AMD +5.0%, QCOM +5.5%, TER +5.9%, KLAC +4.3% — while NVDA sits at −0.04%. The flag-bearer of the AI trade is flat on the sector's best day this week; breadth 46/84 green.
cards.watch: NVDA reclaiming green would broaden the leg — a sector rally without its largest name has a shorter leash.

cards.headline: ADBE −6.7%: CFO departure stacks on the downgrade
cards.body: Day two of the software bleed, and now with a governance angle: RBC flags "more uncertainty" around the fiscal-2026 organic ARR guide after Adobe's CFO departure. Apps & Data −1.35% is again the weakest sector while silicon leads — the out-of-software rotation keeps running.

cards.headline: ARM +8.8%: probe headlines couldn't dent it
cards.body: Follow-through from the morning — FTC-probe coverage is still circulating and ARM leads the universe anyway, with QCOM +5.5% alongside in the licensing/mobile complex. A name that rallies through its own bad-news cycle is telling you where the bid is.

cards.headline: Quiet indices under loud headlines
cards.body: SPY +0.48%, QQQ +0.59%, TLT −0.31% — the index tape is calm while the day's action concentrates in one sector and one IPO. Iran headlines are still flip-flopping (the Geneva-signing denial stands). Concentration is the risk card: if semis cool, today's green tape has little else holding it up.
